articles.title: COMMON CAUSES OF MEMORY LEAKS IN JAVASCRIPT
articles.summary: Memory usage is tracked in Node.js (V8) with metrics like RSS, Heap Total, Heap Used, External, and Array Buffers. There are various causes of memory leaks, such as improperly managed variables, persistent global objects, unremoved event listeners, closures capturing variables, and circular references. Developers can profile memory usage by using the `--prof` flag and analyzing the resulting profile file to identify areas of potential memory leak.

articles.title: HOW WE RUN MIGRATIONS ACROSS 2,800 MICROSERVICES
HOW META ENFORCES PURPOSE LIMITATION VIA PRIVACY AWARE INFRASTRUCTURE AT SCALE
articles.summary: Meta's Privacy Aware Infrastructure (PAI) is a large-scale initiative designed to enforce purpose limitation. PAI's key component, Policy Zones, provides a comprehensive mechanism for encapsulating, evaluating, and propagating privacy constraints for data in transit and at rest. Its integration across various Meta systems, including function-based and batch-processing systems, makes sure that data is only used for its intended purpose.

articles.title: ANYONE ELSE FIND TAILWIND CSS A BIT TOO REDUNDANT? (REDDIT THREAD)
articles.summary: Reddit users debate whether Tailwind is too redundant or not. Some people find the class names to be repetitive and difficult to maintain. Others find Tailwind CSS to be helpful and time-saving.

articles.title: MERCHANTS OF COMPLEXITY
articles.summary: People often prefer complex solutions over simple ones, even though they claim to want simplicity. This is because complex solutions make people feel special and superior. Basic problems are often turned into insurmountable challenges during development, requiring expensive subscriptions for "sophisticated" solutions.

articles.title: [FUSIONAUTH BLOG] DON'T TRUST AN AUTH VENDOR WITHOUT SEEING IT RUN IN YOUR APPLICATION
articles.summary: Authentication is one of the main building blocks of any software product, and you want to be really sure you're making the right choice. The only reliable way to do that is to run it in your actual code [9], in your actual application logic. If there's no free trial,

articles.title: SIMULACRUM
articles.summary: Simulacrum allows you to write code to build interactive diagrams for complex software flows. It's a React component with an IDE, a compiler, and a runtime all working together. The compiler converts your code into something that can emit the program's custom instructions.

articles.title: FREE PUBLIC APIS
articles.summary: A collection of 224 free public APIs for developers that have been verified and tested.

articles.title: BACKGROUND REMOVER
articles.summary: This is a free background remover that uses WebGPU to remove backgrounds locally in-browser.

articles.title: 5,000X FASTER CRDTS: AN ADVENTURE IN OPTIMIZATION
articles.summary: This developer was able to optimize CRDTs (Conflict-Free Replicated Data types) for collaborative editing by 5,000x. They started with a simple data structure change using a flat list instead of a tree. Then they adopted a bidirectional linked list and run-length encoding, leading to a 30x speedup compared to the original Automerge implementation. Finally, by using Rust's memory control capabilities and a range tree data structure, the developer achieved a 5,000x speedup, demonstrating that CRDTs can achieve performance comparable to native strings.

articles.title: LOOMING LIABILITY MACHINES (LLMS)
articles.summary: Using LLMs for root cause analysis (RCA) for cloud incidents is probably not a great idea. LLMs, while capable of automating some aspects of RCA, lack the depth and nuance of human experts. Furthermore, there's the "automation surprise" problem, where unexpected LLM behavior could lead to dangerous situations due to user misunderstanding.

articles.title: ACCURATE PROXY METRIC EVALUATION
articles.summary: Netflix researchers developed methods to analyze historical A/B test data to understand the relationship between proxy metrics and long-term business outcomes. They propose three estimators that offer more robust solutions for understanding the true relationship between proxy metrics and north star metrics: Total Covariance (TC), Jackknife Instrumental Variables Estimation (JIVE), and Limited Information Maximum Likelihood (LIML).

articles.title: TASKWARRIOR
articles.summary: TaskWarrior is free, open-source software that helps you manage your TODOs from the command-line.

articles.title: THE MONOSPACE WEB
articles.summary: Monospace fonts and grid systems can be used to create readable, consistent, and visually appealing web pages.

articles.title: ANTHROPIC PUBLISHES THE ‘SYSTEM PROMPTS' THAT MAKE CLAUDE TICK
articles.summary: Anthropic has published the system prompts used to guide its Claude AI models and plans to continue being transparent moving forward.

articles.title: DOCKER APP HOSTING
articles.summary: This article describes how to use Docker to host applications on a VPS, which is cheaper and often easier to maintain than using traditional cloud providers.
